raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 156 revolutionizes the State’s broadband deployment program under the California Advanced Services Fund Program.",
    "The ongoing COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted significant gaps in internet connectivity at sufficient speeds for many Californians.",
    "Access to the internet is essential for participating in today’s online world.",
    "California has committed to a generational investment in providing access to internet-based services like education, job training, telehealth, and the digital economy.",
    "The investment to modernize California's broadband infrastructure is necessary and overdue.",
    "Expanding access to affordable, high-speed internet will empower future generations and provide better opportunities for upward mobility.",
    "The Broadband Budget Bill prioritizes the deployment of broadband infrastructure in California’s most vulnerable and unserved rural and urban communities.",
    "AB 14 provides a pathway to connect California’s workforce to gainful employment and harness telemedicine technology.",
    "The Act extends eligibility for grants administered by the California Public Utilities Commission to local and tribal governments to connect households and community institutions.",
    "AB 156 aims to build a statewide fiber middle-mile network and improve last-mile connections to homes and businesses, increasing access to higher speed connectivity."
  ]
}
```
